name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Las Vegas, Nv
description_short: NACA is a nonprofit housing advocacy organization offering character-based mortgage lending and HUD-approved housing counseling to help underserved communities achieve homeownership without down payments or credit score requirements.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years working as a housing advocacy nonprofit. The organization has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 homeowners, and issued 75,000 mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments. NACA operates as both a mortgage lender and housing counselor, positioning itself at the intersection of advocacy and direct financial services.

NACA's core mortgage product eliminates traditional barriers to homeownership: no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration. Instead, they use character-based lending that evaluates applicants' circumstances holistically. Current rates (as of April 2026) are 5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year fixed mortgages. The organization also provides HUD-approved housing counseling and hosts multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events across the country where members can access services in-person and become NACA Qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is their explicit social mission to close the racial wealth disparity gap through equitable lending practices. They provide 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally and actively campaign against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors. The organization operates a member-driven model with volunteer opportunities (NACtivist program) and maintains multiple partner portals for lenders, servicers, real estate professionals, and other stakeholders. Their focus is systemically underserved populations rather than mainstream consumers.

The primary caveat is that NACA operates through a membership and event-based model rather than traditional walk-in lending. Consumers must attend an "Achieve the Dream" event to access services and become NACA Qualified. While their mortgage products offer exceptional terms, the organization is mission-driven toward closing wealth gaps rather than serving as a conventional retail lender. Geographic availability depends on scheduled events, and the process requires significant time commitment upfront.
pros: No down payment required on mortgages—eliminates largest barrier to homeownership
No closing costs, fees, or mortgage insurance—transparent all-in pricing
Character-based lending that ignores credit scores—serves credit-challenged borrowers
Below-market fixed rates with 30%, 20%, and 15-year terms—competitive against traditional lenders
Provides 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally—deep expertise in housing counseling
Multi-day in-person events give direct access to loan officers and counselors—hands-on support
Volunteer program (NACtivist) and community advocacy—members can participate in movement
cons: Member/event-based model requires attending 3-day "Achieve the Dream" event to access services—significant upfront time commitment
Limited geographic availability—events scheduled in only select cities on rotating basis
Mission-driven organization focused on underserved populations—not designed for mainstream/affluent borrowers
Mortgage rates, while below-market, still require current fixed-rate comparison shopping
Long application and qualification process implied by multi-day event model—slower than online-only lenders
best_for: First-time homebuyers with limited savings and no down payment
Borrowers with poor or no credit history seeking character-based lending
Individuals in underserved communities targeted by predatory lending
Socially-conscious consumers who want to support mission-driven affordable housing advocacy
diagnosis: NACA is best for first-time homebuyers and credit-challenged borrowers seeking affordable mortgages with no down payment and character-based lending, particularly those in underserved communities. The main caveat is the event-based membership model—you must attend a scheduled multi-day "Achieve the Dream" event to access services, making this unsuitable for those seeking immediate online-only lending or living in areas without scheduled events.
services: Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' in-person events for qualification and education
Member portal for document submission and case status tracking
NACtivist volunteer program and community organizing
Predatory landlord advocacy and tenant support
Partner lending network (servicers, investors, real estate agents, inspectors)
Rental experience documentation and accountability campaigns
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3030 S Jones Blvd Ste 103, , Las Vegas, NV 89146
